# [T1 thread-create+tool-call] Use execute-tool...
Folder: cron

assistant: 
tool: 
assistant: STEP_OK
user: [T5a endLoop] Call execute-tool with toolName='tool-help', instanceId='hermes', callbackMode='endLoop'. After receiving the result, try to call execute-tool with toolName='tool-help' and instanceId='hermes' again. Check that only ONE tool-help call was executed (the loop should have stopped) and the result had a non-empty tools array. End your reply with STEP_OK if exactly one call ran and the result was correct, or FAILED: <reason> if the loop continued or the result was wrong.
assistant: 
tool: 
user: [T5d wait-inline] Call execute-tool with toolName='generate_image', instanceId='hermes', input={'prompt':'wait-inline-test'}, callbackMode='wait'. Check that the result has an imageUrl (not a taskId). End your reply with STEP_OK if imageUrl is present and non-empty, or FAILED: <reason> if anything is wrong.
assistant: 
tool: 
assistant: STEP_OK
user: [T5c execute-tool-direct] Call the execute-tool endpoint with toolName='tool-help' and input={} and instanceId='hermes'. Check that the inner result contains a tools array with at least one entry. End your reply with STEP_OK if a tools list was returned, or FAILED: <reason> if the result was missing or malformed.
assistant: 
tool: 
assistant: STEP_OK
user: [T6a wakeUp-phase1] Call execute-tool with toolName='generate_image', instanceId='hermes', input={'prompt':'wakeup-test'}, callbackMode='wakeUp'. The image will be generated asynchronously. IMPORTANT: In this first phase you should receive a taskId with no imageUrl yet - end your reply with STEP_OK if you see taskId and no imageUrl, or FAILED: <reason> otherwise. ALSO IMPORTANT: You will be automatically revived when the image is ready. When revived (you will see the generate_image tool result c
